title: AI Won't Replace Engineers — It Will Expose Bad Ones
excerpt: The fear isn't about automation. It's about accountability. AI doesn't replace the engineer — it removes the excuse.
stance: AI tools raise the bar for engineering quality, they don't lower the headcount.
counterView: If the skill floor rises fast enough, junior engineers without fundamentals may find the gap insurmountable before they can build them.
body:
const{Fragment:e,jsx:n,jsxs:t}=arguments[0];function _createMdxContent(i){const r={em:"em",h2:"h2",p:"p",strong:"strong",...i.components};return t(e,{children:[t(r.p,{children:["Every few months, the discourse resurfaces: ",n(r.em,{children:"AI is going to replace software engineers."})]}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"I think this gets the causality backwards."}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"The real pressure isn't replacement"}),"
",t(r.p,{children:["AI tools — Copilot, Claude, Cursor — are extraordinarily good at generating boilerplate, suggesting implementations, and reducing the friction of writing obvious code. What they cannot do is ",n(r.strong,{children:"define the problem correctly"}),"."]}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"The engineer's job has always been, at its core, a translation job: take an ambiguous human need, convert it into a precise specification, and implement it in a system that will fail in ways no one anticipated."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"AI doesn't do that. It does the third part reasonably well, when the first two parts have already been done."}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"What AI actually exposes"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"Consider an engineer who relied on their ability to write CRUD endpoints quickly. That skill is now table stakes — anyone with a good prompt can do it. The differentiator has shifted."}),"
",t(r.p,{children:["The engineers who will be under pressure aren't the ones doing deep systems work. They're the ones whose primary value was ",n(r.strong,{children:"producing code volume"}),". AI removes the friction that made volume impressive."]}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"This is not a threat. It's a clarification."}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"The floor is rising, not the ceiling"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"The engineering ceiling — the complexity of problems that require genuine systems thinking, security intuition, domain expertise, and architectural judgment — hasn't changed. What's changed is the floor."}),"
",t(r.p,{children:["Five years ago, you could build a career on implementation speed. That's harder now. The floor is code that ",n(r.em,{children:"works"}),". The new floor is code that ",n(r.em,{children:"should exist"}),"."]}),"
",n(r.h2,{children:"Where I might be wrong"}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"If the floor rises faster than junior engineers can build fundamentals, the path from beginner to competent engineer may become steeper, not easier. That's a real concern — not about AI replacing engineers, but about AI compressing the time available to develop judgment."}),"
",n(r.p,{children:"The tool is neutral. The impact depends on what we do with the time it buys us."})]})}return{default:function(e={}){const{wrapper:t}=e.components||{};return t?n(t,{...e,children:n(_createMdxContent,{...e})}):_createMdxContent(e)}};
